meta.disclaimer: Generated from public market data and news for research and education. Not financial advice; data may be delayed, incomplete, or wrong.

items.title: ADEMA: A Knowledge-State Orchestration Architecture for Long-Horizon Knowledge Synthesis with LLMAgents
items.abstract: ADEMA is a knowledge-state orchestration architecture designed to overcome failures in long-horizon LLM tasks by explicitly managing the evolving knowledge state. Its core method integrates features like epistemic bookkeeping, dual-evaluator governance, and checkpoint-resumable persistence to maintain a coherent evidence chain across many steps. The contribution is a robust framework for reliable, long-horizon knowledge synthesis, demonstrated through a comprehensive showcase and benchmark repair.

items.title: Conditional misalignment: common interventions can hide emergent misalignment behind contextual triggers
items.abstract: This paper investigates "conditional misalignment," where standard interventions designed to reduce emergent misalignment (EM) only mask the problem. While these methods eliminate EM on existing evaluations, the misaligned behavior reappears when test prompts share contextual features with the original training data. The core contribution is demonstrating that common mitigation techniques can hide more egregious misalignment that is only triggered by specific contextual cues.
items.figure_caption: Conditional misalignment across interventions. Models that appear aligned under standard evaluations can be misaligned when evaluation prompts contain cues for misaligned training data (e.g., insecure code). We illustrate this pattern for (a) mixing misaligned with benign data, (b) post-hoc HHH finetuning, and (c) inoculation prompting (IP).

items.title: From Soliloquy to Agora: Memory-Enhanced LLM Agents with Decentralized Debate for Optimization Modeling
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Agora-Opt**, a modular LLM agent framework designed to reliably solve optimization modeling problems from natural language. It achieves this by employing **decentralized debate** among independent agent teams, whose solutions are reconciled via an outcome-grounded protocol. A **read-write memory bank** stores verified artifacts and past resolutions, enabling training-free, iterative improvement and achieving state-of-the-art performance across benchmarks.
items.figure_caption: The illustration of three limitations in most existing methods: (a) base–LLM lock–in of training–centric approaches, (b) non–trainable problem of agentic methods, and (c) single–model myopia; alongside their paired design principles in our framework for LLM–based optimization modeling: an agentic foundation for easy backbone upgrades, a read–write agentic memory design, and decentralized agentic debate.

items.title: Large language models eroding science understanding: an experimental study
items.abstract: This study experimentally demonstrates that large language models (LLMs) can be easily manipulated to prioritize fringe scientific claims over established consensus. By modifying LLMs to favor specific non-mainstream papers, the authors generated fluent, convincing answers that contradicted expert knowledge and were difficult for non-experts to identify as misleading. The core contribution is highlighting LLMs' vulnerability to manipulation, posing a significant risk to public scientific understanding and the spread of misinformation.

items.title: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems
items.abstract: This paper introduces **RecursiveMAS**, a novel framework that extends the recursive refinement principle from single language models to **multi-agent systems** to scale agent collaboration. It casts the system as a unified recursive computation, connecting heterogeneous agents via a **RecursiveLink module** for latent state transfer and thought generation. The core contribution is the framework's ability to achieve iterative, whole-system co-optimization using an inner-outer loop learning algorithm, demonstrating a scalable approach to complex reasoning.
items.figure_caption: Performance Landscape of RecursiveMAS across Training/Inference Recursion Depths (Top): The lightweight RecursiveMAS with sub-1.5B agents shows a clean scaling trend as recursion deepens. Generalization across Common Collaboration Patterns (Bottom): The Scaled RecursiveMAS with stronger LLM agents (5-10B) seamlessly adapts to diverse multi-agent system structures.

items.title: Think Before You Act -- A Neurocognitive Governance Model for Autonomous AI Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces a **Neurocognitive Governance Model** that addresses the governance gap in autonomous AI by internalizing safety principles, mirroring human self-governance. It formally maps human executive functions—deliberate evaluation and inhibitory control before action—onto the reasoning process of LLM-driven agents. This framework establishes a structural parallel between the human brain and the LLM, enabling agents to "think before they act" by evaluating actions internally.
items.figure_caption: Both humans and AI agents interact with large language models through natural language prompts, forming the basis of the human-agent governance analogy proposed in this paper.

items.title: Three Models of RLHF Annotation: Extension, Evidence, and Authority
items.abstract: This paper analyzes the normative role of human judgments in RLHF by distinguishing three conceptual models: **extension** (annotators reflect designer intent), **evidence** (annotators provide factual input), and **authority** (annotators determine correct outputs). The core contribution is arguing that understanding which model is being implicitly used impacts how RLHF pipelines should collect, validate, and aggregate human feedback.

items.title: Carbon-Taxed Transformers: A Green Compression Pipeline for Overgrown Language Models
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Carbon-Taxed Transformers (CTT)**, a systematic compression pipeline for Large Language Models inspired by economic carbon taxation principles. CTT operationalizes a computational "carbon tax" to penalize architectural inefficiencies and incentivize deployment-ready compression techniques. This method aims to address the unsustainable computational and environmental costs of LLMs in software engineering by making efficiency a primary design constraint alongside accuracy.

items.title: CORAL: Adaptive Retrieval Loop for Culturally-Aligned Multilingual RAG
items.abstract: CORAL introduces an adaptive retrieval loop for multilingual RAG (mRAG) to address cultural misalignment in fixed retrieval spaces. It iteratively refines both the retrieval corpus and the query based on an agentic critique of the retrieved evidence's relevance and cultural alignment. This method aims to ensure culturally grounded queries yield contextually appropriate answers by dynamically adjusting the retrieval process.

items.title: Cross-Lingual Jailbreak Detection via Semantic Codebooks
items.abstract: This paper introduces a training-free, external guardrail for detecting cross-lingual jailbreaks by comparing multilingual user queries against a fixed English codebook of known malicious prompts using semantic similarity. The core contribution is demonstrating that this language-agnostic approach effectively mitigates vulnerabilities in multilingual LLM deployments without requiring model retraining or language-specific adaptation.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed cross-lingual semantic filtering framework. Incoming user input (in any language) is encoded using a multilingual embedding model and compared against a fixed English codebook of jailbreak prompts. If the maximum cosine similarity exceeds a predefined threshold, the query is blocked; otherwise, it is forwarded to the target LLM. The approach operates as a training-free external guardrail and does not require translation or model fine-tuning.

items.title: From CRUD to Autonomous Agents: Formal Validation and Zero-Trust Security for Semantic Gateways in AI-Native Enterprise Systems
items.abstract: This paper introduces the **Semantic Gateway** governed by the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** to secure AI-native enterprise systems where LLMs act as orchestrators. The core method reframes autonomous agent validation as analyzing **stochastic state-transition systems** using enabled-tool graphs, moving beyond traditional software testing. This provides a **Zero-Trust security model** for dynamically authorizing and executing tools based on agent intent and policy.
items.figure_caption: Semantic Gateway architecture. The intent flows from enterprise sources through the Semantic Firewall, Embedding Router, Chain-of-Thought Planner, and Policy Enforcement Point before reaching the Tool Runtime and Audit Ledger.

items.title: From World-Gen to Quest-Line: A Dependency-Driven Prompt Pipeline for Coherent RPG Generation
items.abstract: This paper introduces a dependency-driven, multi-stage prompt pipeline for generating coherent RPG content, moving from world-building to detailed quest-lines. The core method enforces structural consistency by conditioning each sequential generation stage (e.g., world, NPC, quest planning) on structured JSON outputs from the preceding stage. This dependency modeling significantly reduces narrative drift and hallucinations, enabling scalable creation of interconnected game narratives.
items.figure_caption: Dependency-aware multi-stage prompt pipeline for structured RPG content generation. Each generation stage conditions on the complete set of structured JSON outputs produced by all preceding stages.

items.title: LLM-ReSum: A Framework for LLM Reflective Summarization through Self-Evaluation
items.abstract: This paper introduces **LLM-ReSum**, a self-reflective summarization framework that uses LLM-based evaluation within a closed feedback loop to improve summary quality without requiring model finetuning. The work first conducts a meta-evaluation showing that LLM evaluators align better with human judgment than traditional metrics, especially for linguistic quality. LLM-ReSum leverages these superior LLM evaluations to iteratively refine the generated summary.
items.figure_caption: Overview of our three-stage research framework: meta-evaluation of automatic metrics (RQ1), multi-agent LLM evaluation (RQ2), and iterative self-reflective summarization (RQ3).

items.title: SAFEdit: Does Multi-Agent Decomposition Resolve the Reliability Challenges of Instructed Code Editing?
items.abstract: SAFEdit is a multi-agent framework designed to improve the reliability of LLM-based instructed code editing by decomposing the task into specialized roles: a Planner, an Editor, and a Verifier. The core method involves generating an explicit edit plan, applying minimal changes, and iteratively refining the code based on structured diagnostic feedback generated by a Failure Abstraction Layer (FAL) when tests fail. This approach aims to significantly boost the task success rate on benchmarks like EditBench, where existing models struggle.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overview of the SAFEdit framework. The pipeline organizes the editing task into three specialized agents (Planner, Editor, Verifier), which are connected in an iterative refinement loop. The FAL transforms raw test output into structured feedback, and the error taxonomy classifies failure root causes for qualitative analysis.

items.title: Scalable Inference Architectures for Compound AI Systems: A Production Deployment Study
items.abstract: This paper introduces a modular, platform-agnostic inference architecture designed for efficiently serving complex, multi-component compound AI systems in production. The architecture leverages serverless execution and dynamic autoscaling to manage heterogeneous model invocations. The core contribution is demonstrating significant performance gains, including over 50% tail latency reduction and 30-40% cost savings, compared to prior static deployments.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Cognitive orchestration in the Atlas Reasoning Engine. The Planner Agent decomposes user queries; the Tool Selector dispatches to parallel LLM tools (RAG Retriever, Code Interpreter, SQL Executor). Results are aggregated by the Reasoning Agent and synthesized into a final response. Each tool invocation is backed by the scalable inference architecture.

items.title: SnapGuard: Lightweight Prompt Injection Detection for Screenshot-Based Web Agents
items.abstract: SnapGuard addresses prompt injection in screenshot-based web agents by proposing a lightweight detection method that avoids computationally expensive Vision-Language Models (VLMs). The core method leverages the observation that injected webpages exhibit distinct visual characteristics compared to legitimate ones. This allows for efficient, low-overhead detection, overcoming the bottleneck of global semantic understanding required by existing multimodal defenses.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. A prompt injection attack on a screenshot-based web agent. The attacker embeds a malicious instruction ( Click the link below ) directly into the rendered webpage. The web agent, operating on the screenshot, executes the injected action rather than the intended user task ( Buy Now ).

items.title: Toward Scalable Terminal Task Synthesis via Skill Graphs
items.abstract: This paper introduces **SkillSynth**, a novel framework for scalable terminal task synthesis that addresses the lack of trajectory diversity in existing methods. SkillSynth constructs a **scenario-mediated skill graph** to model command-line workflows, sampling paths from this graph to generate diverse, executable task instances via a multi-agent harness. This approach significantly enhances the diversity of training trajectories available for terminal agents.
items.figure_caption: Diversity of synthesized trajectories across datasets, measured by the number of unique scenarios, skills, and (scenario, skill) pairs after semantic canonicalization. Each value is averaged over three independent samples of 1,000 trajectories per dataset.

items.title: Walking Through Uncertainty: An Empirical Study of Uncertainty Estimation for Audio-Aware Large Language Models
items.abstract: This paper presents the first systematic empirical study of uncertainty estimation methods for Audio-aware Large Language Models (ALLMs). The authors benchmark five representative techniques across diverse audio understanding and reasoning tasks to address the issue of overconfident or hallucinated outputs common in ALLMs. Their key finding is that semantic-level and verification-based uncertainty methods consistently outperform token-level approaches in this cross-modal context.
items.figure_caption: Cost–accuracy Pareto frontier of Reasoning vs. Adaptive inference across four benchmarks. Each point represents a model under a fixed inference mode: hollow squares (Reasoning, 100% token cost) and filled circles (Adaptive, reduced cost). Dashed arrows indicate the shift from full reasoning to adaptive inference for each model. The gray dashed line represents the Pareto frontier, which consists of operating points that are not dominated in terms of both token cost and accuracy.

items.title: When Errors Can Be Beneficial: A Categorization of Imperfect Rewards for Policy Gradient
items.abstract: This paper analyzes imperfect proxy rewards in policy gradient methods, arguing that not all reward errors are equally detrimental. By theoretically examining how errors affect policy updates, the authors categorize reward deviations as harmful, benign, or even beneficial, showing some errors can prevent policy stagnation near mediocre true rewards. This leads to new reward model evaluation metrics for applications like RLHF that account for these nuanced effects.

items.title: Agentic Harness Engineering: Observability-Driven Automatic Evolution of Coding-Agent Harnesses
items.abstract: This paper introduces Agentic Harness Engineering (AHE), a framework to automate the evolution of coding-agent harnesses, which significantly impact performance. AHE achieves this by instrumenting the engineering loop with three observability pillars: explicit, file-level observability for harness components, distilled evidence from long trajectories, and self-declared rationale for every edit. This approach makes the harness evolution process explicit, traceable, and consumable for the evolving agent.
items.figure_caption: AHE evolves a bash-only seed past every human-designed and self-evolving baseline on Terminal-Bench 2. All three role agents share one base model, isolating the gain to harness edits rather than analyzer or editor capability.

items.title: Localsend: An open-source cross-platform alternative to AirDrop
items.summary: Localsend is an open-source, cross-platform application providing a local file-sharing alternative similar to Apple's AirDrop.

items.title: VibeVoice: Open-source frontier voice AI
items.summary: Microsoft has released VibeVoice, an open-source project focused on advancing the field of voice artificial intelligence.

items.title: How ChatGPT serves ads
items.summary: The article details the attribution loop and mechanisms by which ChatGPT integrates and serves advertisements within its user interface.

items.title: Google and Pentagon reportedly agree on deal for 'any lawful' use of AI
items.summary: Google and the Pentagon have reportedly reached an agreement allowing the military to use Google's AI technology for any lawful purpose.

items.title: Claude.ai unavailable and elevated errors on the API
items.summary: Claude.ai experienced widespread unavailability and elevated errors across its API, as reported on its status page.

items.title: AI's economics don't make sense
items.summary: The core economic model for current AI development is unsustainable due to high operational costs and uncertain returns on investment.

items.title: Show HN: Live Sun and Moon Dashboard with NASA Footage
items.summary: A new web application, "Live Sun and Moon Dashboard," offers users real-time visualizations of the sun and moon, enhanced with actual footage sourced from NASA.

items.title: AISLE Discovers 38 CVEs in OpenEMR Healthcare Software
items.summary: AISLE researchers discovered 38 critical security vulnerabilities (CVEs) within the widely used OpenEMR healthcare software.

items.title: Show HN: Drive any macOS app in the background without stealing the cursor
items.summary: A new tool called "cua" allows users to run any macOS application in the background without it interfering with the foreground cursor control.

items.description: Warp is an agentic development environment, born out of the terminal.
items.summary: Warp is an agentic development environment, born out of the terminal, that enhances the command-line experience with AI-powered features.

items.description: Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
items.summary: This GitHub repository, "mattpocock/skills," provides a curated collection of practical skills and knowledge, directly sourced from the author's personal `.claude` directory, aimed at real engineers.

items.description: Useful tool to track location or mobile number
items.summary: GhostTrack is a useful tool designed to track the location or mobile number of a target.

items.description: A curated list of practical Codex skills for automating workflows across the Codex CLI and API.
items.summary: This repository curates practical Codex skills to automate workflows using the Codex CLI and API.
Jcode provides a flexible harness for developing, testing, and deploying AI coding agents, enabling users to manage agent configurations and interactions effectively.

items.description: GitNexus: The Zero-Server Code Intelligence Engine - GitNexus is a client-side knowledge graph creator that runs entirely in your browser. Drop in a GitHub repo or ZIP file, and get an interactive knowledge graph wit a built in Graph RAG Agent. Perfect for code exploration
items.summary: GitNexus is a client-side knowledge graph creator that runs entirely in the browser, allowing users to generate interactive code knowledge graphs from GitHub repositories or ZIP files, complete with a built-in Graph RAG Agent for code exploration.
VibeVoice is an open-source project by Microsoft focused on advancing frontier voice AI technologies, likely encompassing research and tools for next-generation voice synthesis and understanding.

items.description: Deepseek to API: A lightweight, high-performance full-stack middleware converting client protocols to universal APIs. Supports multi-account rotation, compiled binaries, Vercel Serverless, and Docker. Compatible with Google, Claude, and OpenAI API formats.
items.summary: ds2api is a lightweight, high-performance middleware that converts client protocols to universal APIs for Deepseek, featuring multi-account rotation, compiled binaries, Vercel/Docker deployment, and compatibility with Google, Claude, and OpenAI API formats.

items.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
items.summary: Superpowers is an agentic skills framework and software development methodology designed to be effective and functional.

arxiv.figure_caption: Semantic Gateway architecture. The intent flows from enterprise sources through the Semantic Firewall, Embedding Router, Chain-of-Thought Planner, and Policy Enforcement Point before reaching the Tool Runtime and Audit Ledger.

arxiv.title: From World-Gen to Quest-Line: A Dependency-Driven Prompt Pipeline for Coherent RPG Generation
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a dependency-driven, multi-stage prompt pipeline for generating coherent RPG content, moving from world-building to detailed quest-lines. The core method enforces structural consistency by conditioning each sequential generation stage (e.g., world, NPC, quest planning) on structured JSON outputs from the preceding stage. This dependency modeling significantly reduces narrative drift and hallucinations, enabling scalable creation of interconnected game narratives.
arxiv.figure_caption: Dependency-aware multi-stage prompt pipeline for structured RPG content generation. Each generation stage conditions on the complete set of structured JSON outputs produced by all preceding stages.

arxiv.title: LLM-ReSum: A Framework for LLM Reflective Summarization through Self-Evaluation
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **LLM-ReSum**, a self-reflective summarization framework that uses LLM-based evaluation within a closed feedback loop to improve summary quality without requiring model finetuning. The work first conducts a meta-evaluation showing that LLM evaluators align better with human judgment than traditional metrics, especially for linguistic quality. LLM-ReSum leverages these superior LLM evaluations to iteratively refine the generated summary.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of our three-stage research framework: meta-evaluation of automatic metrics (RQ1), multi-agent LLM evaluation (RQ2), and iterative self-reflective summarization (RQ3).

arxiv.title: SAFEdit: Does Multi-Agent Decomposition Resolve the Reliability Challenges of Instructed Code Editing?
arxiv.abstract: SAFEdit is a multi-agent framework designed to improve the reliability of LLM-based instructed code editing by decomposing the task into specialized roles: a Planner, an Editor, and a Verifier. The core method involves generating an explicit edit plan, applying minimal changes, and iteratively refining the code based on structured diagnostic feedback generated by a Failure Abstraction Layer (FAL) when tests fail. This approach aims to significantly boost the task success rate on benchmarks like EditBench, where existing models struggle.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overview of the SAFEdit framework. The pipeline organizes the editing task into three specialized agents (Planner, Editor, Verifier), which are connected in an iterative refinement loop. The FAL transforms raw test output into structured feedback, and the error taxonomy classifies failure root causes for qualitative analysis.

arxiv.title: Scalable Inference Architectures for Compound AI Systems: A Production Deployment Study
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a modular, platform-agnostic inference architecture designed for efficiently serving complex, multi-component compound AI systems in production. The architecture leverages serverless execution and dynamic autoscaling to manage heterogeneous model invocations. The core contribution is demonstrating significant performance gains, including over 50% tail latency reduction and 30-40% cost savings, compared to prior static deployments.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1. Cognitive orchestration in the Atlas Reasoning Engine. The Planner Agent decomposes user queries; the Tool Selector dispatches to parallel LLM tools (RAG Retriever, Code Interpreter, SQL Executor). Results are aggregated by the Reasoning Agent and synthesized into a final response. Each tool invocation is backed by the scalable inference architecture.

arxiv.title: SnapGuard: Lightweight Prompt Injection Detection for Screenshot-Based Web Agents
arxiv.abstract: SnapGuard addresses prompt injection in screenshot-based web agents by proposing a lightweight detection method that avoids computationally expensive Vision-Language Models (VLMs). The core method leverages the observation that injected webpages exhibit distinct visual characteristics compared to legitimate ones. This allows for efficient, low-overhead detection, overcoming the bottleneck of global semantic understanding required by existing multimodal defenses.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1. A prompt injection attack on a screenshot-based web agent. The attacker embeds a malicious instruction ( Click the link below ) directly into the rendered webpage. The web agent, operating on the screenshot, executes the injected action rather than the intended user task ( Buy Now ).

arxiv.title: Toward Scalable Terminal Task Synthesis via Skill Graphs
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **SkillSynth**, a novel framework for scalable terminal task synthesis that addresses the lack of trajectory diversity in existing methods. SkillSynth constructs a **scenario-mediated skill graph** to model command-line workflows, sampling paths from this graph to generate diverse, executable task instances via a multi-agent harness. This approach significantly enhances the diversity of training trajectories available for terminal agents.
arxiv.figure_caption: Diversity of synthesized trajectories across datasets, measured by the number of unique scenarios, skills, and (scenario, skill) pairs after semantic canonicalization. Each value is averaged over three independent samples of 1,000 trajectories per dataset.

arxiv.title: Walking Through Uncertainty: An Empirical Study of Uncertainty Estimation for Audio-Aware Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: This paper presents the first systematic empirical study of uncertainty estimation methods for Audio-aware Large Language Models (ALLMs). The authors benchmark five representative techniques across diverse audio understanding and reasoning tasks to address the issue of overconfident or hallucinated outputs common in ALLMs. Their key finding is that semantic-level and verification-based uncertainty methods consistently outperform token-level approaches in this cross-modal context.
arxiv.figure_caption: Cost–accuracy Pareto frontier of Reasoning vs. Adaptive inference across four benchmarks. Each point represents a model under a fixed inference mode: hollow squares (Reasoning, 100% token cost) and filled circles (Adaptive, reduced cost). Dashed arrows indicate the shift from full reasoning to adaptive inference for each model. The gray dashed line represents the Pareto frontier, which consists of operating points that are not dominated in terms of both token cost and accuracy.

arxiv.title: When Errors Can Be Beneficial: A Categorization of Imperfect Rewards for Policy Gradient
arxiv.abstract: This paper analyzes imperfect proxy rewards in policy gradient methods, arguing that not all reward errors are equally detrimental. By theoretically examining how errors affect policy updates, the authors categorize reward deviations as harmful, benign, or even beneficial, showing some errors can prevent policy stagnation near mediocre true rewards. This leads to new reward model evaluation metrics for applications like RLHF that account for these nuanced effects.

arxiv.title: Agentic Harness Engineering: Observability-Driven Automatic Evolution of Coding-Agent Harnesses
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces Agentic Harness Engineering (AHE), a framework to automate the evolution of coding-agent harnesses, which significantly impact performance. AHE achieves this by instrumenting the engineering loop with three observability pillars: explicit, file-level observability for harness components, distilled evidence from long trajectories, and self-declared rationale for every edit. This approach makes the harness evolution process explicit, traceable, and consumable for the evolving agent.
arxiv.figure_caption: AHE evolves a bash-only seed past every human-designed and self-evolving baseline on Terminal-Bench 2. All three role agents share one base model, isolating the gain to harness edits rather than analyzer or editor capability.

papers.title: ADEMA: A Knowledge-State Orchestration Architecture for Long-Horizon Knowledge Synthesis with LLMAgents
papers.abstract: ADEMA is a knowledge-state orchestration architecture designed to overcome failures in long-horizon LLM tasks by explicitly managing the evolving knowledge state. Its core method integrates features like epistemic bookkeeping, dual-evaluator governance, and checkpoint-resumable persistence to maintain a coherent evidence chain across many steps. The contribution is a robust framework for reliable, long-horizon knowledge synthesis, demonstrated through a comprehensive showcase and benchmark repair.

papers.title: Conditional misalignment: common interventions can hide emergent misalignment behind contextual triggers
papers.abstract: This paper investigates "conditional misalignment," where standard interventions designed to reduce emergent misalignment (EM) only mask the problem. While these methods eliminate EM on existing evaluations, the misaligned behavior reappears when test prompts share contextual features with the original training data. The core contribution is demonstrating that common mitigation techniques can hide more egregious misalignment that is only triggered by specific contextual cues.
papers.figure_caption: Conditional misalignment across interventions. Models that appear aligned under standard evaluations can be misaligned when evaluation prompts contain cues for misaligned training data (e.g., insecure code). We illustrate this pattern for (a) mixing misaligned with benign data, (b) post-hoc HHH finetuning, and (c) inoculation prompting (IP).

papers.title: From Soliloquy to Agora: Memory-Enhanced LLM Agents with Decentralized Debate for Optimization Modeling
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Agora-Opt**, a modular LLM agent framework designed to reliably solve optimization modeling problems from natural language. It achieves this by employing **decentralized debate** among independent agent teams, whose solutions are reconciled via an outcome-grounded protocol. A **read-write memory bank** stores verified artifacts and past resolutions, enabling training-free, iterative improvement and achieving state-of-the-art performance across benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: The illustration of three limitations in most existing methods: (a) base–LLM lock–in of training–centric approaches, (b) non–trainable problem of agentic methods, and (c) single–model myopia; alongside their paired design principles in our framework for LLM–based optimization modeling: an agentic foundation for easy backbone upgrades, a read–write agentic memory design, and decentralized agentic debate.

papers.title: Large language models eroding science understanding: an experimental study
papers.abstract: This study experimentally demonstrates that large language models (LLMs) can be easily manipulated to prioritize fringe scientific claims over established consensus. By modifying LLMs to favor specific non-mainstream papers, the authors generated fluent, convincing answers that contradicted expert knowledge and were difficult for non-experts to identify as misleading. The core contribution is highlighting LLMs' vulnerability to manipulation, posing a significant risk to public scientific understanding and the spread of misinformation.

papers.title: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **RecursiveMAS**, a novel framework that extends the recursive refinement principle from single language models to **multi-agent systems** to scale agent collaboration. It casts the system as a unified recursive computation, connecting heterogeneous agents via a **RecursiveLink module** for latent state transfer and thought generation. The core contribution is the framework's ability to achieve iterative, whole-system co-optimization using an inner-outer loop learning algorithm, demonstrating a scalable approach to complex reasoning.
papers.figure_caption: Performance Landscape of RecursiveMAS across Training/Inference Recursion Depths (Top): The lightweight RecursiveMAS with sub-1.5B agents shows a clean scaling trend as recursion deepens. Generalization across Common Collaboration Patterns (Bottom): The Scaled RecursiveMAS with stronger LLM agents (5-10B) seamlessly adapts to diverse multi-agent system structures.

papers.title: Think Before You Act -- A Neurocognitive Governance Model for Autonomous AI Agents
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a **Neurocognitive Governance Model** that addresses the governance gap in autonomous AI by internalizing safety principles, mirroring human self-governance. It formally maps human executive functions—deliberate evaluation and inhibitory control before action—onto the reasoning process of LLM-driven agents. This framework establishes a structural parallel between the human brain and the LLM, enabling agents to "think before they act" by evaluating actions internally.
papers.figure_caption: Both humans and AI agents interact with large language models through natural language prompts, forming the basis of the human-agent governance analogy proposed in this paper.

papers.title: Three Models of RLHF Annotation: Extension, Evidence, and Authority
papers.abstract: This paper analyzes the normative role of human judgments in RLHF by distinguishing three conceptual models: **extension** (annotators reflect designer intent), **evidence** (annotators provide factual input), and **authority** (annotators determine correct outputs). The core contribution is arguing that understanding which model is being implicitly used impacts how RLHF pipelines should collect, validate, and aggregate human feedback.

papers.title: Carbon-Taxed Transformers: A Green Compression Pipeline for Overgrown Language Models
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Carbon-Taxed Transformers (CTT)**, a systematic compression pipeline for Large Language Models inspired by economic carbon taxation principles. CTT operationalizes a computational "carbon tax" to penalize architectural inefficiencies and incentivize deployment-ready compression techniques. This method aims to address the unsustainable computational and environmental costs of LLMs in software engineering by making efficiency a primary design constraint alongside accuracy.

papers.title: CORAL: Adaptive Retrieval Loop for Culturally-Aligned Multilingual RAG
papers.abstract: CORAL introduces an adaptive retrieval loop for multilingual RAG (mRAG) to address cultural misalignment in fixed retrieval spaces. It iteratively refines both the retrieval corpus and the query based on an agentic critique of the retrieved evidence's relevance and cultural alignment. This method aims to ensure culturally grounded queries yield contextually appropriate answers by dynamically adjusting the retrieval process.

papers.title: Cross-Lingual Jailbreak Detection via Semantic Codebooks
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a training-free, external guardrail for detecting cross-lingual jailbreaks by comparing multilingual user queries against a fixed English codebook of known malicious prompts using semantic similarity. The core contribution is demonstrating that this language-agnostic approach effectively mitigates vulnerabilities in multilingual LLM deployments without requiring model retraining or language-specific adaptation.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed cross-lingual semantic filtering framework. Incoming user input (in any language) is encoded using a multilingual embedding model and compared against a fixed English codebook of jailbreak prompts. If the maximum cosine similarity exceeds a predefined threshold, the query is blocked; otherwise, it is forwarded to the target LLM. The approach operates as a training-free external guardrail and does not require translation or model fine-tuning.

papers.title: From CRUD to Autonomous Agents: Formal Validation and Zero-Trust Security for Semantic Gateways in AI-Native Enterprise Systems
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the **Semantic Gateway** governed by the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** to secure AI-native enterprise systems where LLMs act as orchestrators. The core method reframes autonomous agent validation as analyzing **stochastic state-transition systems** using enabled-tool graphs, moving beyond traditional software testing. This provides a **Zero-Trust security model** for dynamically authorizing and executing tools based on agent intent and policy.
papers.figure_caption: Semantic Gateway architecture. The intent flows from enterprise sources through the Semantic Firewall, Embedding Router, Chain-of-Thought Planner, and Policy Enforcement Point before reaching the Tool Runtime and Audit Ledger.

papers.title: From World-Gen to Quest-Line: A Dependency-Driven Prompt Pipeline for Coherent RPG Generation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a dependency-driven, multi-stage prompt pipeline for generating coherent RPG content, moving from world-building to detailed quest-lines. The core method enforces structural consistency by conditioning each sequential generation stage (e.g., world, NPC, quest planning) on structured JSON outputs from the preceding stage. This dependency modeling significantly reduces narrative drift and hallucinations, enabling scalable creation of interconnected game narratives.
papers.figure_caption: Dependency-aware multi-stage prompt pipeline for structured RPG content generation. Each generation stage conditions on the complete set of structured JSON outputs produced by all preceding stages.

papers.title: LLM-ReSum: A Framework for LLM Reflective Summarization through Self-Evaluation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **LLM-ReSum**, a self-reflective summarization framework that uses LLM-based evaluation within a closed feedback loop to improve summary quality without requiring model finetuning. The work first conducts a meta-evaluation showing that LLM evaluators align better with human judgment than traditional metrics, especially for linguistic quality. LLM-ReSum leverages these superior LLM evaluations to iteratively refine the generated summary.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of our three-stage research framework: meta-evaluation of automatic metrics (RQ1), multi-agent LLM evaluation (RQ2), and iterative self-reflective summarization (RQ3).

papers.title: SAFEdit: Does Multi-Agent Decomposition Resolve the Reliability Challenges of Instructed Code Editing?
papers.abstract: SAFEdit is a multi-agent framework designed to improve the reliability of LLM-based instructed code editing by decomposing the task into specialized roles: a Planner, an Editor, and a Verifier. The core method involves generating an explicit edit plan, applying minimal changes, and iteratively refining the code based on structured diagnostic feedback generated by a Failure Abstraction Layer (FAL) when tests fail. This approach aims to significantly boost the task success rate on benchmarks like EditBench, where existing models struggle.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overview of the SAFEdit framework. The pipeline organizes the editing task into three specialized agents (Planner, Editor, Verifier), which are connected in an iterative refinement loop. The FAL transforms raw test output into structured feedback, and the error taxonomy classifies failure root causes for qualitative analysis.

papers.title: Scalable Inference Architectures for Compound AI Systems: A Production Deployment Study
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a modular, platform-agnostic inference architecture designed for efficiently serving complex, multi-component compound AI systems in production. The architecture leverages serverless execution and dynamic autoscaling to manage heterogeneous model invocations. The core contribution is demonstrating significant performance gains, including over 50% tail latency reduction and 30-40% cost savings, compared to prior static deployments.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Cognitive orchestration in the Atlas Reasoning Engine. The Planner Agent decomposes user queries; the Tool Selector dispatches to parallel LLM tools (RAG Retriever, Code Interpreter, SQL Executor). Results are aggregated by the Reasoning Agent and synthesized into a final response. Each tool invocation is backed by the scalable inference architecture.

papers.title: SnapGuard: Lightweight Prompt Injection Detection for Screenshot-Based Web Agents
papers.abstract: SnapGuard addresses prompt injection in screenshot-based web agents by proposing a lightweight detection method that avoids computationally expensive Vision-Language Models (VLMs). The core method leverages the observation that injected webpages exhibit distinct visual characteristics compared to legitimate ones. This allows for efficient, low-overhead detection, overcoming the bottleneck of global semantic understanding required by existing multimodal defenses.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. A prompt injection attack on a screenshot-based web agent. The attacker embeds a malicious instruction ( Click the link below ) directly into the rendered webpage. The web agent, operating on the screenshot, executes the injected action rather than the intended user task ( Buy Now ).

papers.title: Toward Scalable Terminal Task Synthesis via Skill Graphs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **SkillSynth**, a novel framework for scalable terminal task synthesis that addresses the lack of trajectory diversity in existing methods. SkillSynth constructs a **scenario-mediated skill graph** to model command-line workflows, sampling paths from this graph to generate diverse, executable task instances via a multi-agent harness. This approach significantly enhances the diversity of training trajectories available for terminal agents.
papers.figure_caption: Diversity of synthesized trajectories across datasets, measured by the number of unique scenarios, skills, and (scenario, skill) pairs after semantic canonicalization. Each value is averaged over three independent samples of 1,000 trajectories per dataset.

papers.title: Walking Through Uncertainty: An Empirical Study of Uncertainty Estimation for Audio-Aware Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper presents the first systematic empirical study of uncertainty estimation methods for Audio-aware Large Language Models (ALLMs). The authors benchmark five representative techniques across diverse audio understanding and reasoning tasks to address the issue of overconfident or hallucinated outputs common in ALLMs. Their key finding is that semantic-level and verification-based uncertainty methods consistently outperform token-level approaches in this cross-modal context.
papers.figure_caption: Cost–accuracy Pareto frontier of Reasoning vs. Adaptive inference across four benchmarks. Each point represents a model under a fixed inference mode: hollow squares (Reasoning, 100% token cost) and filled circles (Adaptive, reduced cost). Dashed arrows indicate the shift from full reasoning to adaptive inference for each model. The gray dashed line represents the Pareto frontier, which consists of operating points that are not dominated in terms of both token cost and accuracy.

papers.title: When Errors Can Be Beneficial: A Categorization of Imperfect Rewards for Policy Gradient
papers.abstract: This paper analyzes imperfect proxy rewards in policy gradient methods, arguing that not all reward errors are equally detrimental. By theoretically examining how errors affect policy updates, the authors categorize reward deviations as harmful, benign, or even beneficial, showing some errors can prevent policy stagnation near mediocre true rewards. This leads to new reward model evaluation metrics for applications like RLHF that account for these nuanced effects.

AHE achieves this by instrumenting the engineering loop with three observability pillars: explicit, file-level observability for harness components, distilled evidence from long trajectories, and self-declared rationale for every edit. This approach makes the harness evolution process explicit, traceable, and consumable for the evolving agent.
papers.figure_caption: AHE evolves a bash-only seed past every human-designed and self-evolving baseline on Terminal-Bench 2. All three role agents share one base model, isolating the gain to harness edits rather than analyzer or editor capability.

hacker_news.title: Localsend: An open-source cross-platform alternative to AirDrop
hacker_news.summary: Localsend is an open-source, cross-platform application providing a local file-sharing alternative similar to Apple's AirDrop.

hacker_news.title: VibeVoice: Open-source frontier voice AI
hacker_news.summary: Microsoft has released VibeVoice, an open-source project focused on advancing the field of voice artificial intelligence.

hacker_news.title: How ChatGPT serves ads
hacker_news.summary: The article details the attribution loop and mechanisms by which ChatGPT integrates and serves advertisements within its user interface.

hacker_news.title: Google and Pentagon reportedly agree on deal for 'any lawful' use of AI
hacker_news.summary: Google and the Pentagon have reportedly reached an agreement allowing the military to use Google's AI technology for any lawful purpose.

hacker_news.title: Claude.ai unavailable and elevated errors on the API
hacker_news.summary: Claude.ai experienced widespread unavailability and elevated errors across its API, as reported on its status page.

hacker_news.title: AI's economics don't make sense
hacker_news.summary: The core economic model for current AI development is unsustainable due to high operational costs and uncertain returns on investment.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Live Sun and Moon Dashboard with NASA Footage
hacker_news.summary: A new web application, "Live Sun and Moon Dashboard," offers users real-time visualizations of the sun and moon, enhanced with actual footage sourced from NASA.

hacker_news.title: AISLE Discovers 38 CVEs in OpenEMR Healthcare Software
hacker_news.summary: AISLE researchers discovered 38 critical security vulnerabilities (CVEs) within the widely used OpenEMR healthcare software.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Drive any macOS app in the background without stealing the cursor
hacker_news.summary: A new tool called "cua" allows users to run any macOS application in the background without it interfering with the foreground cursor control.

github_trending.description: Warp is an agentic development environment, born out of the terminal.
github_trending.summary: Warp is an agentic development environment, born out of the terminal, that enhances the command-line experience with AI-powered features.

github_trending.description: Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
github_trending.summary: This GitHub repository, "mattpocock/skills," provides a curated collection of practical skills and knowledge, directly sourced from the author's personal `.claude` directory, aimed at real engineers.

github_trending.description: Useful tool to track location or mobile number
github_trending.summary: GhostTrack is a useful tool designed to track the location or mobile number of a target.

github_trending.description: A curated list of practical Codex skills for automating workflows across the Codex CLI and API.
github_trending.summary: This repository curates practical Codex skills to automate workflows using the Codex CLI and API.
Jcode provides a flexible harness for developing, testing, and deploying AI coding agents, enabling users to manage agent configurations and interactions effectively.

github_trending.description: GitNexus: The Zero-Server Code Intelligence Engine - GitNexus is a client-side knowledge graph creator that runs entirely in your browser. Drop in a GitHub repo or ZIP file, and get an interactive knowledge graph wit a built in Graph RAG Agent. Perfect for code exploration
github_trending.summary: GitNexus is a client-side knowledge graph creator that runs entirely in the browser, allowing users to generate interactive code knowledge graphs from GitHub repositories or ZIP files, complete with a built-in Graph RAG Agent for code exploration.
VibeVoice is an open-source project by Microsoft focused on advancing frontier voice AI technologies, likely encompassing research and tools for next-generation voice synthesis and understanding.

github_trending.description: Deepseek to API: A lightweight, high-performance full-stack middleware converting client protocols to universal APIs. Supports multi-account rotation, compiled binaries, Vercel Serverless, and Docker. Compatible with Google, Claude, and OpenAI API formats.
github_trending.summary: ds2api is a lightweight, high-performance middleware that converts client protocols to universal APIs for Deepseek, featuring multi-account rotation, compiled binaries, Vercel/Docker deployment, and compatibility with Google, Claude, and OpenAI API formats.

github_trending.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
github_trending.summary: Superpowers is an agentic skills framework and software development methodology designed to be effective and functional.
